LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CONNIE CILETTI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN THAT (1) DEFENDANT IS AN ORIGINAL TENANT, AND (2) DEFENDANT IS A TENANT WHOSE OCCUPANCY PLAINTIFF CONSENTED TO. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT BY RELYING ON DEFENDANT DEPOSITION TESIMONY OR THE TSANG DECLARATION BECAUSE THOSE DO NOT OVERCOME PARK MERCED, 215 CAL.APP.3D 490, AND SAN FRANCISCO RENT RULE 6.14, AS THOSE APPLY TO THE FACTS HERE. COURT RULED ON PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE, ALL OVERRULED; DEFENDANT'S ONE OBJECTION, OVERRULED. IN ADDITION, THE COURT INDICATED ITS RULING WAS BASED ON BOTH ISSUE NO. 1 AND ISSUE NO. 2 IN DEFENDANT'S SEPARATE STATEMENT, AND ALSO BASED ITS RULING ON THE 2000 VERION OF RULE 6.14. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II, REPORTER: JOYCE LONG, CSR #8620
MINI-MINUTES FOR FEB-18-2010 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - CONTINUED TO OCTOBER 20, 2010 AT 10:00AM IN DEPARTMENT 502, TO BE HEARD BEFORE JUDGE ROBERTSON. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Off Calendar May-10-2010 Continued To Jul-16-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-05-2010 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-18-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CONNIE CILETTI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: Jak Marquez, Esq. & Curtis F. Dowling, Esq. of Beckman Marquez & Dowling, LLP 495-8500 for pltf & opposing party; Daphne C. Lin, Esq. of Trump, Alioto, Trump & Prescott, LLP (510)790-0900/(415)563-7200 for deft & moving party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - granted. Defendant shifted the burden that (1) Defendant is an original tenant, and (2) Defendant is a tenant whose occupancy Plaintiff consented to. Plaintiff fails to create a triable issue of fact by relying on defendant deposition tesimony or the Tsang declaration because those do not overcome Park Merced, 215 Cal.App.3d 490, and San Francisco Rent Rule 6.14, as those apply to the facts here. Court ruled on plaintiff's objections to evidence, all overruled; defendant's one objection, overruled. In addition, the Court indicated its ruling was based on both issue no. 1 and issue no. 2 in defendant's separate statement, and also based its ruling on the 2000 verion of rule 6.14. (302/AJR) JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II, REPORTER: JOYCE LONG, CSR# 8620

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-14-2009 Set For Trial On Mar-22-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Mar-05-10 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 604
